Cucumber/Core Mechanics/Data Tables and.description: Cucumber provides Data Tables and Examples to handle data-driven scenarios effectively. Choosing the correct mechanism helps maintain readability, maintainability, and proper BDD design.

Cucumber/Core Mechanics/Hooks Before Aft.id: Cucumber/Core Mechanics/Hooks Before After and Best Practices
Cucumber/Core Mechanics/Hooks Before Aft.title: Hooks - Before, After, and Best Practices
Cucumber/Core Mechanics/Hooks Before Aft.description: Hooks in Cucumber allow execution of code before or after scenarios. They are commonly used for test setup and teardown, such as launching browsers, initializing test data, and cleaning resources.

Cucumber/Core Mechanics/Parameterization.id: Cucumber/Core Mechanics/Parameterization and Data Mapping in Step Definitions
Cucumber/Core Mechanics/Parameterization.title: Parameterization and Data Mapping in Step Definitions
Cucumber/Core Mechanics/Parameterization.description: Parameterization allows Cucumber steps to accept dynamic data instead of hardcoded values. This makes scenarios more reusable, flexible, and easier to maintain.

Cucumber/Core Mechanics/Shared State and.id: Cucumber/Core Mechanics/Shared State and Scenario Context
Cucumber/Core Mechanics/Shared State and.title: Shared State and Scenario Context
Cucumber/Core Mechanics/Shared State and.description: In real Cucumber frameworks, steps often need to share data.

Cucumber/Core Mechanics/Step Definition.title: Step Definition Basics
Cucumber/Core Mechanics/Step Definition.description: Step definitions map Gherkin steps to executable automation code. They act as the bridge between readable .feature files and the actual test implementation.

Cucumber/Core Mechanics/Tags and Tag Exp.title: Tags & Tag Expressions
Cucumber/Core Mechanics/Tags and Tag Exp.description: Tags in Cucumber allow you to control which scenarios are executed without modifying feature files. They are essential for selective execution, managing large test suites, and integrating with CI/CD pipelines.

Cucumber/Execution & Integration/Cucumbe.id: Cucumber/Execution & Integration/Cucumber with API Automation Rest Assured Patterns
Cucumber/Execution & Integration/Cucumbe.title: Cucumber with API Automation Rest Assured
Cucumber/Execution & Integration/Cucumbe.description: Using Cucumber with Rest Assured enables behavior‑driven API automation. Proper architecture ensures tests remain scalable, reusable, and maintainable.

Cucumber/Execution & Integration/Cucumbe.id: Cucumber/Execution & Integration/Cucumber with Selenium UI Automation Patterns
Cucumber/Execution & Integration/Cucumbe.title: Cucumber with Selenium UI Automation Pattern
Cucumber/Execution & Integration/Cucumbe.description: When using Cucumber for UI automation, structuring Selenium code correctly is essential. Following clean architecture patterns ensures that automation frameworks remain scalable, maintainable, and readable.

Cucumber/Execution & Integration/Cucumbe.title: Cucumber with TestNG Integration
Cucumber/Execution & Integration/Cucumbe.description: Cucumber focuses on Behavior Driven Development (BDD) and scenario execution, but it relies on test frameworks like TestNG to manage execution control, lifecycle handling, and parallel execution.

Cucumber/Execution & Integration/cucumbe.title: Cucumber Across Languages
Cucumber/Execution & Integration/cucumbe.description: Cucumber supports multiple programming languages, making it a powerful tool for teams working across diverse technology stacks. While feature files remain the same, step definitions and implementation logic can be written in different programming languages.

Cucumber/Execution & Integration/debuggi.title: Debugging Cucumber Tests
Cucumber/Execution & Integration/debuggi.description: Debugging is an essential skill when working with Cucumber frameworks. Tests may fail due to incorrect step definitions, misconfigured runners, flaky UI interactions, or shared state issues.

Cucumber/Execution & Integration/Paralle.title: Parallel Execution in Cucumber
Cucumber/Execution & Integration/Paralle.description: Parallel execution is essential for reducing execution time in large automation suites. However, incorrect parallel configuration can introduce flaky tests if shared state and thread safety are not handled properly.

Cucumber/Execution & Integration/Runner.id: Cucumber/Execution & Integration/Runner Class and Cucumber Options
Cucumber/Execution & Integration/Runner.title: Runner Class and Cucumber Options
Cucumber/Execution & Integration/Runner.description: The Runner Class is responsible for triggering Cucumber test execution. It defines what to run, where to find it, and how results should be reported.

Cucumber/Foundations/from-user-stories-t.title: From User Stories to Feature Files
Cucumber/Foundations/from-user-stories-t.description: In Behavior-Driven Development (BDD), user stories form the foundation for writing feature files. Translating user stories into Gherkin scenarios ensures that tests align with business requirements and acceptance criteria.

Cucumber/Foundations/Gherkin Language Ba.title: Gherkin Language Basics
Cucumber/Foundations/Gherkin Language Ba.description: Gherkin is a domain-specific language used by Cucumber to describe application behavior in a human-readable format. It allows teams to write scenarios that are understandable by business stakeholders, testers, and developers alike.

Cucumber/Foundations/Introduction.title: Introduction to Cucumber
Cucumber/Foundations/Introduction.description: Learn what Cucumber is, why teams use it, and how it fits into automation testing.

Cucumber/Foundations/What is BDD.title: What is BDD?
Cucumber/Foundations/What is BDD.description: Behavior-Driven Development (BDD) is a development and testing approach that focuses on system behavior from the user’s perspective, rather than technical implementation details.

Cucumber/Foundations/Writing Good Gherki.title: Writing Good Gherkin Scenarios
Cucumber/Foundations/Writing Good Gherki.description: Writing good Gherkin scenarios is both an art and a science. While the syntax of Gherkin is simple, crafting scenarios that are clear, concise, and maintainable requires careful thought and collaboration.

Cucumber/Operational Excellence/Common C.id: Cucumber/Operational Excellence/Common Cucumber Framework Anti Patterns
Cucumber/Operational Excellence/Common C.title: Common Cucumber Framework Anti-Patterns
Cucumber/Operational Excellence/Common C.description: Anti-patterns are practices that appear to work initially but create long-term problems in automation frameworks. Understanding these mistakes helps teams design stable and maintainable Cucumber frameworks.

Cucumber/Operational Excellence/Cucumber.title: Cucumber Architecture Overview
Cucumber/Operational Excellence/Cucumber.description: Understanding Cucumber architecture is important before building automation frameworks. A well‑designed architecture improves maintainability, scalability, and debugging.

Cucumber/Operational Excellence/Cucumber.id: Cucumber/Operational Excellence/Cucumber in CI CD Pipelines
Cucumber/Operational Excellence/Cucumber.title: Cucumber in CI/CD Pipelines
Cucumber/Operational Excellence/Cucumber.description: Integrating Cucumber tests into CI/CD pipelines provides fast feedback, continuous validation, and release confidence.

Cucumber/Operational Excellence/Error Ha.id: Cucumber/Operational Excellence/Error Handling Retries and Flaky Test Control
Cucumber/Operational Excellence/Error Ha.title: Error Handling, Retries & Flaky Test Control
Cucumber/Operational Excellence/Error Ha.description: Flaky tests reduce trust in automation frameworks and disrupt CI/CD pipelines. This section explains how to handle failures properly, apply retries carefully, and design stable Cucumber frameworks.

Cucumber/Operational Excellence/handling.title: Handling Secrets and Sensitive Data
Cucumber/Operational Excellence/handling.description: Managing secrets securely is critical in automation frameworks. Credentials, API tokens, and database connections must never be exposed in source code or logs.

Cucumber/Operational Excellence/Reportin.title: Reporting and Plugins
Cucumber/Operational Excellence/Reportin.description: Reporting is a critical part of Cucumber automation. Reports help teams understand test results, debug failures, and track quality trends, especially in CI/CD pipelines.

Extent Report/advanced_configuration_and.title: Advanced Configuration & Customization
Extent Report/advanced_configuration_and.description: This section covers controlled customization of Extent Reports used in real-time enterprise projects. The goal is clarity and traceability—not visual overengineering.

Extent Report/ci_cd_integration.title: CI/CD Integration
Extent Report/ci_cd_integration.description: This section explains how Extent Reports is used in real CI/CD pipelines such as Jenkins and GitLab. A report that works locally but fails in CI is considered broken.

Extent Report/common_mistakes_and_anti_p.title: Common Mistakes & Anti-Patterns
Extent Report/common_mistakes_and_anti_p.description: This section documents real mistakes repeatedly seen in production automation frameworks. Avoiding these is as important as knowing the correct implementation.

Extent Report/creenshots_and_media_handl.title: Screenshots & Media Handling
Extent Report/creenshots_and_media_handl.description: Screenshots are non-negotiable in real-time automation projects. This section explains how to capture and attach screenshots correctly so they remain accurate under parallel execution and CI pipelines.

Extent Report/cucumber_bdd_integration.title: Cucumber / BDD Integration
Extent Report/cucumber_bdd_integration.description: This section explains how Extent Reports integrates with Cucumber-based BDD frameworks in real-time projects. The goal is to produce business-readable reports without breaking thread safety or framework discipline.

Extent Report/framework_level_integratio.title: Framework-Level Integration
Extent Report/framework_level_integratio.description: This section explains how Extent Reports fits into a real automation framework. At this level, reporting is no longer a utility—it becomes a stable infrastructure component.

Extent Report/fundamentals_and_architect.title: Fundamentals & Core Architecture
Extent Report/fundamentals_and_architect.description: This section explains how Extent Reports actually works internally and why many implementations fail in real-time projects. Understanding this architecture prevents flaky, incorrect, and untrustworthy reports.

Extent Report/logging_and_status_handlin.title: Logging & Status Handling
Extent Report/logging_and_status_handlin.description: This section explains how to log correctly in Extent Reports so reports remain readable, accurate, and trustworthy in real-time projects. Bad logging practices are one of the biggest reasons reports become noisy and useless.

Extent Report/roadmap_overview.title: Extent Reports – Real‑Time Roadmap (Overview)
Extent Report/roadmap_overview.description: This roadmap is validated against real enterprise automation frameworks using Selenium, TestNG, Cucumber, Jenkins, and GitLab. It focuses on what is actually implemented in production, not demo‑level usage.

Extent Report/setup_and_basic_configurat.title: Setup & Basic Configuration
Extent Report/setup_and_basic_configurat.description: This section shows how to set up Extent Reports correctly so it works reliably in real-time projects, including CI pipelines and parallel execution. Version mismatch and bad initialization are the #1 causes of reporting issues—this avoids both.

Extent Report/test_ng_listener_integrati.title: TestNG Listener Integration
Extent Report/test_ng_listener_integrati.description: This section explains how TestNG listeners orchestrate Extent Reports correctly in real-time automation frameworks. In production projects, listeners—not test classes—own the reporting lifecycle.

Extent Report/thread_safety_and_parallel.title: Thread Safety & Parallel Execution
Extent Report/thread_safety_and_parallel.description: This section is the most critical part of Extent Reports in real-time projects. Without proper thread safety, reports become unreliable under parallel execution—even if tests themselves pass.

Jenkins/Agents & Scaling/docker-agents.description: Docker agents allow Jenkins to run builds inside ephemeral containers.

Jenkins/Agents & Scaling/kubernetes-agen.description: Kubernetes agents allow Jenkins to run builds as ephemeral pods inside a Kubernetes cluster.

Jenkins/Architecture/executors.description: Executors control how many jobs run in parallel in Jenkins.

Jenkins/Architecture/master-slave-archit.description: This document explains the core execution model of Jenkins.

Jenkins/Architecture/overview.description: This section introduces the core architectural concepts of Jenkins.
